risk_if_not_implemented: Without Privileged Account Identifiers, unauthorized users may gain access to systems or data, increasing risk of data breaches and insider threats.

compensating_control_1.name: Identifier Management (User Names)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to govern naming standards for usernames and Technology Assets, Applications and/or Services (TAAS).
compensating_control_1.justification: Identifier Management (User Names) (IAC-09) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Privileged Account Identifiers (IAC-09.5) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Authenticator Management
compensating_control_2.description:
Mechanisms exist to:
(1) Securely manage authenticators for users and devices; and
(2) Ensure the strength of authentication is appropriate to the classification of the data being accessed.
compensating_control_2.justification: Authenticator Management (IAC-10) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Privileged Account Identifiers (IAC-09.5) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
